name: plc_document_verification
description: Canonical Dina-flavoured PLC DID document. Pinning the field shape, context set, verification-method fragment (#dina_signing), messaging-service fragment (#dina_messaging), and Multikey encoding. Shape-level only — Ed25519 / secp256k1 signature verification is out-of-scope for this vector (that lands in task 10.11 / crypto-backed vectors).

producer.tool: Hand-assembled to match @dina/core/src/identity/did_document.ts output shape; conforming to packages/protocol/src/types/plc_document.ts.

notes: publicKeyMultibase uses multibase base58-btc encoding (z-prefix). The 34-byte payload for Ed25519 is: multicodec prefix 0xed 0x01 (2 bytes) followed by 32 bytes of raw Ed25519 public key.

The #dina_signing fragment (underscore form) is the publish-time canonical; readers MUST also accept the legacy #dina-signing (hyphen) form for backward compat with pre-0.14 Dina builds. Same backward-compat rule applies to #dina_messaging.
service[].type is one of two literal strings: DinaMsgBox (for MsgBox-relayed D2D) or DinaDirectHTTPS (for direct-to-home-node over HTTPS). Anything else is non-conforming.

did:plc identifier body is 24 or more lowercase alphanumeric characters (Bluesky's PLC spec — base32 over the truncated SHA-256 of the genesis record). The example 'example7alonso7alice012345ab' is a test fixture and not a real registered DID.
authentication[] is a list of verification-method URIs. Each entry MUST be the full URL form (DID + fragment), not just the fragment.

The document is JSON-serialised with arbitrary key order — unlike DinaMessage, the PLC document is a W3C DID doc and consumers (Bluesky infra, AT Protocol relays) accept any key ordering. Dina's emitter picks a stable order for readability but does not depend on it for signatures.
